title: plagiobothrys stipitatus var. stipitatus
intro: Plagiobothrys stipitatus var. stipitatus, also known as Wild Heliotrope or Beach Heliotrope, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Boraginaceae. It is a perennial herb native to the coastal regions of the Pacific Northwest in North America, known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory qualities. The plant has been used to treat a range of health issues, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are commonly used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically to aid in wound healing and internally to alleviate respiratory issues.
